result:
Experiment designed, pre-registered, and implemented. Summary of this iteration:

**Diagnosis.** The kept best (exp-2, 2489 m) sits barely under the ~3217 m center-guess floor, and exp-3, exp-4, and exp-5 each rewrote the supervision/decode side and failed the same way — the probability field stays near-uniform. Reading the frozen `pipeline/dataset.py` against `model/train.py` revealed an untouched suspect on the data side: training extracts each of the few hundred train-grid locations **once, at one fixed random heading, into a static tensor reused for every epoch**, while the frozen eval rotates every crop by a different deterministic angle. The model is never forced to recognize a place from more than one heading, so eval-time rotations produce features it has never seen, the field stays flat, and the soft-argmax decode collapses to the map center.

**Experiment (`runs/pending_experiment.json`).** "Per-epoch rotation resampling replaces the one-shot frozen training tensor" — category `augmentation`, from-scratch init. The one focused change, in `model/train.py` only: the old loader is split into a one-time source load (meta, train-crop grid, six relight images cached in RAM) and a per-epoch sampler that re-runs `extract_crop` with a fresh uniform-random angle for every (location, bucket) pair. Locations stay on the frozen `list_crops` grid, so the no-eval-leakage guarantee is untouched; `model/model.py`, the loss, and the exported inference path are unchanged, so the architecture diagram only gains a train-only stage.

**Expected outcome.** If rotation overfit is the binding constraint, worst-case median error drops clearly below 2489 m (plausibly 1400–2300 m). Downside is bounded near no-change: same locations, same model, and a uniform field still decodes to the map center.

Both modules import cleanly and the pending-experiment JSON parses. The harness can now train, score, and decide keep/revert.
